name: What proof do I need if I fell at work and no one saw?
description: Med records, photos, your statement—attorney gets more evidence.

mainEntity.name: What proof do I need if I fell at work and no one saw?

acceptedAnswer.text: Med records, photos, your statement—attorney gets more evidence.
acceptedAnswer.description: **Your testimony, medical records, photos of hazard, and maintenance logs suffice even without witnesses.** Circumstantial evidence proves work-related slip and falls in Utah/Idaho workers' comp.[1] Key factors: (1) Doctor notes linking injury to fall; (2) Supervisor report; (3) Prior complaints about area. Preserve shoes/clothing. An attorney obtains employer records via discovery.
